b' Trump-Biden Predictions, Politico News & G.O.P. Ad Wars
Welcome back to The Best & The Brightest. Happy debate night! Finally, the candidates are being put to the test on the stage together again, four years older, with plenty more scars and facing all the same hazards, except heightened. I can say affirmatively that everyone is holding their breath. Today, I reveal the whispers around town about a debate that could dramatically change the stakes of an election that has been a dead heat for over a year. \xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c\xc2\xa0\xe2\x80\x8c

Welcome back to The Best & The Brightest. I\xe2\x80\x99m Tara Palmeri .

Happy debate night! Finally, the candidates are being put to the test on the stage together again, four years older, with plenty more scars and facing all the same hazards, except heightened. I can say affirmatively that everyone is holding their breath. Today, I reveal the whispers around town about a debate that could dramatically change the stakes of an election that has been a dead heat for over a year.

\xf0\x9f\x8e\xa7 Programming note : I have a rollicking episode of Somebody\xe2\x80\x99s Gotta Win with The Bulwark\xe2\x80\x99s Marc Caputo that\xe2\x80\x99s a solid debate primer, laying out our fresh reporting on what the candidates are being advised, their mental states, the pitfalls, the stakes, and of course, our predictions.

But first\xe2\x80\xa6 Here\xe2\x80\x99s Dylan Byers with an update on the other media story in town\xe2\x80\xa6

  • Politico\xe2\x80\x99s prince : For well over a year now, Politico founding editor John Harris and his heir apparent Alex Burns have been trying to turn the once-scrappy and then long-complacent news outfit into a truly professional, world-class news organization, befitting their owner Axel Springer\xe2\x80\x99s ambition of becoming the \xe2\x80\x9cleading digital publisher in democracies around the world.\xe2\x80\x9d The effort has met with considerable friction in the newsroom, causing some journalists to butt heads with management\xe2\x80\x94most notably Burns, whom some view as an overbearing editor. Now, as Semafor reports , several journalists are heading toward the exit: Alex Ward and Lara Seligman (to The Wall Street Journal ), Sam Stein (to The Bulwark), Burgess Everett (to Semafor), and Jack Shafer (to be determined).

    The most notable losses here are Ward and Seligman, whose moves signal that Politico is not yet world-class enough to keep its promising young talent from defecting, which has always been a challenge for the organization. (On the flip side, the moves signal the Journal \xe2\x80\x99s continued commitment to Washington coverage despite the recent shake-up of its D.C. bureau). As for the others, well, Harris only barely tried to hide his view that they weren\xe2\x80\x99t the right fit for Politico\xe2\x80\x99s new iteration: \xe2\x80\x9cI want people to find the right home for their work, even if sometimes it is elsewhere.\xe2\x80\x9d In any event, while some of these folks no doubt had very real gripes with leadership, they mostly seem to have been about creative control of the copy (and, apparently, a few instances last year in which Burns stealth-edited some of that copy). Ultimately, these intra-office squabbles don\xe2\x80\x99t have much bearing on Politico\xe2\x80\x99s ability to mature the business. As Harris notes, there are still 600 journalists there who haven\xe2\x80\x99t left. \xe2\x80\x94 Dylan Byers

And now, Abby Livingston has the latest on the political ad wars\xe2\x80\xa6
The N.R.C.C.\xe2\x80\x99s TV Explosion
The National Republican Congressional Committee announced its ad reservations today\xe2\x80\x94typically the final step taken by each party\xe2\x80\x99s House campaign arm to publicize their TV spending plans before November. They revealed a surprising aggressiveness from the House G.O.P., which spent money in 22 districts, 13 of which are held by Democrats. The main takeaway, however, is that it seems that neither party believes a House wave is coming; Republicans and Democrats are on offense and defense; and that this fall is shaping up to be a district-by-district knife fight.

  • Topline takeaways : The N.R.C.C. has reserved $44 million in advertising, compared with the D.C.C.C.\xe2\x80\x99s $28 million. These partisan groups work hand-in-hand with super PACs, and so far, in aggregate, House Democrats have locked in around $214 million in reservations versus House Republicans\xe2\x80\x99 $187 million. Looking at only the PACs, the Democratic-aligned House Majority PAC has reserved $186 million compared with the G.O.P.-aligned Congressional Leadership Fund\xe2\x80\x99s $141 million.

  • Filling the gaps : Even more money will be spent when candidates deploy their own war chests to secure the bargain candidate rate for TV ads. Super PACs, of course, are not allowed to directly coordinate with candidates or the House committees, and so these groups often communicate with each other by releasing their ad-booking information to the press. Meanwhile, outside groups focused on a particular issue or candidate demographics then look to fill the gaps in TV markets during various weeks on the calendar.

  • The Oregon trail : Perhaps the N.R.C.C.\xe2\x80\x99s most telling reservation is the $6 million it spent in the Portland, Oregon, media market. For comparison, the next-highest reservation is $2.5 million in the Los Angeles market. This shouldn\xe2\x80\x99t be read as Republicans prioritizing Oregon over Southern California\xe2\x80\x94the Congressional Leadership Fund has reserved $18 million in Los Angeles. But what it does say is that freshman Oregon Republican Lori Chavez-DeRemer \xe2\x80\x99s reelection is being viewed as a top priority, as is targeting Democratic freshman Marie Gluesenkamp Perez in adjacent Washington state.

  • Dem targets : Because district lines overlap in the same media market, it\xe2\x80\x99s typically challenging to determine what money is targeting which seats. But the N.R.C.C. has explicitly stated it\xe2\x80\x99s focused on the five Democrats whose districts Donald Trump won in 2020: Gluesenkamp Perez, plus Alaska\xe2\x80\x99s Mary Peltola , Maine\xe2\x80\x99s Jared Golden , Ohio\xe2\x80\x99s Marcy Kaptur , and Pennsylvania\xe2\x80\x99s Matt Cartwright .

    Finally, both the N.R.C.C. and the C.L.F. are avoiding the Las Vegas market for now. Three Democratic incumbents\xe2\x80\x94 Steven Horsford , Susie Lee , and Dina Titus \xe2\x80\x94are in races which are rated Likely Democratic by The Cook Political Report. This has alleviated some of the pressure on Vegas ad rates, a welcome development for ad buyers in the tightly contested Senate and presidential races.
A Trump-Biden Debate Pre-Mortem
I chatted with some of the top operatives around town to get their honest read and predictions on tonight\xe2\x80\x99s first, and possibly only, presidential debate. Here\xe2\x80\x99s what people in this town are whispering under their breath.
TARA PALMERI
There\xe2\x80\x99s a palpable enthusiasm in political circles, \xc3\xa0 la 2016, ahead of tonight\xe2\x80\x99s historic presidential debate between Joe Biden and Donald Trump \xe2\x80\x94the first time that a current and former Oval Office holder have engaged in televised battle about their competing visions for the nation. And given that this election season has coalesced around Trump\xe2\x80\x99s legal headaches, the January 6 plot, Biden\xe2\x80\x99s son\xe2\x80\x99s own legal troubles, and questions about their advanced ages, tonight\xe2\x80\x99s CNN debate promises to be dramatic , or at least less depressing than the rest of the cycle.

The debate, itself, is almost certain to break viewership ratings for the network, particularly given its various spellbinding cliffhangers: Will Trump interrupt Biden a zillion times, even with his mic turned off, or go apeshit over his recent conviction? Will Biden have a senior moment? Will Trump? Will CNN\xe2\x80\x99s Jake Tapper and Dana Bash simply stand by while the former president uncorks one of his provably false bot mots? It\xe2\x80\x99s the stuff of great TV. Unfortunately, the future of democracy may hang on some of these points, too.

I spent the past week polling my source base, both Democrats and Republicans, for their predictions, assessments, and expectations. Notably, a number of them suggested that each candidate could win by not losing \xe2\x80\x94a strategy perhaps best described as showing enough restraint to let the opposition self-immolate and thereby define the choice in a voter\xe2\x80\x99s mind.
\xe2\x80\x9cIt\xe2\x80\x99s a 90-minute debate. If Donald Trump talked for 20 minutes, he would win the debate,\xe2\x80\x9d one G.O.P. operative told me. \xe2\x80\x9cTrump just can\xe2\x80\x99t be an asshole the way he was last time. If he can by some miracle do that, we\xe2\x80\x99ll be looking good.\xe2\x80\x9d

A Democratic operative agreed with this cynical advice. \xe2\x80\x9cBoth camps have this insane mentality that they will make the other side look terrible,\xe2\x80\x9d this person told me. \xe2\x80\x9cMost candidates in most campaigns walk in saying, I want to be viewed as the best candidate and I want to look presidential on TV. Now it\xe2\x80\x99s just, I just need to make the other one look crazy or dumb .\xe2\x80\x9d

One Democrat who advises the White House suggested that Biden would win the debate if he simply impressed upon voters, one way or another, that he and Trump were the only candidates running\xe2\x80\x94that neither Gavin Newsom nor Michelle Obama nor Oprah or The Rock were going to descend from the heavens at the last minute. Indeed, on some level, this comports with the White House\xe2\x80\x99s overarching strategy of portraying this election as not simply a referendum on Biden, but on his opponent as well\xe2\x80\x94a sticky wicket for a sitting president, but one that has been made more achievable given Trump\xe2\x80\x99s repeated comments about running the government like a dictatorship (at least on Day One). \xe2\x80\x9cI\xe2\x80\x99m sure Biden will be fine, like the State of the Union. As long as he\xe2\x80\x99s cogent and speaks well, he\xe2\x80\x99ll pass his test,\xe2\x80\x9d a G.O.P. operative said. \xe2\x80\x9cAs for Trump, he has to be smart, a little charming, responsible, and if he can, act like a grown-up.\xe2\x80\x9d

But if Biden does nothing during CNN\xe2\x80\x99s 90-minute showdown other than debunk the misapprehension among 17 percent of independents and even 12 percent of Democrats that he was responsible for ending Roe v. Wade , according to a recent NBC poll, it will be worth it. Indeed, this may be his only shot to address the broader nation in primetime. The Democratic National Convention overlaps with the Olympics, after all. Also, it\xe2\x80\x99s not implausible that Trump or Biden back out of ABC\xe2\x80\x99s planned debate on September 10.

Democrats are hoping Trump repeats his mistakes from the first 2020 debate, when he interrupted both Biden and moderator Chris Wallace ad nauseam . But they also recall Biden\xe2\x80\x99s imprecision with data points, which could be weaponized this time around. \xe2\x80\x9cIf they spent all of this time in Camp David trying to hammer in those numbers into him, every one of those people should be fucking fired,\xe2\x80\x9d said one Democratic operative. \xe2\x80\x9cIf he messes up some stat, then Trump gets to go, \xe2\x80\x98Look at this fucking idiot, he can\xe2\x80\x99t even remember numbers.\xe2\x80\x99 Biden shouldn\xe2\x80\x99t mention a single statistic, because he fumbles it, and people don\xe2\x80\x99t get it anyway. He should talk like he used to do, from the heart, talk about the average American, talk about his family struggles.\xe2\x80\x9d
\xe2\x80\x9cAn Absolute Food Fight Disaster\xe2\x80\x9d
Despite the known unknowns in this debate, each campaign\xe2\x80\x99s arguments are fairly circumscribed. Biden will portray his opponent as an insurrectionist, a felon who is running to avoid a prison term, and a threat to women's rights. Trump, of course, will go after Biden on immigration, inflation, crime, and the wars in Ukraine and Gaza, with the hope, according to his aides, that Biden\xe2\x80\x99s well-known defensiveness about his record will knock him off balance. \xe2\x80\x9cIf Biden is able to get away with a debate, and he doesn\xe2\x80\x99t fuck it up, and Trump can\xe2\x80\x99t get a lead, the election becomes a referendum on Donald,\xe2\x80\x9d said a former Trump advisor. \xe2\x80\x9cAnd that\xe2\x80\x99s not what Donald wants. If Trump is not in the lead after the debate, Trump will lose.\xe2\x80\x9d

In pursuing these arguments, of course, Trump\xe2\x80\x99s famous lack of restraint could risk making him come off as a madman. But some within his camp saw signs of newfound discipline at his rally in Pennsylvania on Saturday night. One advisor told me that he seemed to be in \xe2\x80\x9chappy warrior\xe2\x80\x9d mode, going light on the grievances.

Trump also seems to recognize that the first 2020 debate was a nightmare. His 145 interruptions resulted in a poll and momentum swing for Biden, and one from which his campaign never recovered. \xe2\x80\x9cPeople tell me I did better in the second debate,\xe2\x80\x9d he\xe2\x80\x99s been telling people recently, according to an advisor. \xe2\x80\x9cHe\xe2\x80\x99s clearly heard that he needs to be Donald Trump 2.0,\xe2\x80\x9d this person reiterated. \xe2\x80\x9cThe question is, Does he say fuck it and not do it?\xe2\x80\x9d

Trump, who views debate prep as a sign of weakness and a way for others to take credit for his success, has been doing \xe2\x80\x9cpolicy refreshers\xe2\x80\x9d with advisors like Kellyanne Conway and veep hopefuls, such as J.D. Vance and Marco Rubio . \xe2\x80\x9cHe\xe2\x80\x99d rather it go well, and have other people say he didn\xe2\x80\x99t do debate prep,\xe2\x80\x9d said another Trump confidant. \xe2\x80\x9cI think he needs to be patient and not take the bait and not let things get to him. Let Biden talk, and let him talk himself into a pretzel, and let him go on and on and on. Initially, Trump will, then he won\xe2\x80\x99t be able to handle the attacks, and he\xe2\x80\x99ll want to fight back.\xe2\x80\x9d

Several advisors said that CNN\xe2\x80\x99s plan to cut the microphones when there\xe2\x80\x99s too much crosstalk, or when a candidate\xe2\x80\x99s time is up, might help Trump by reminding him when he\xe2\x80\x99s rambling and prevent any gonzo talk about thorny subjects such as January 6 or America\xe2\x80\x99s epidemic of weak water pressure or the threat posed by sharks. \xe2\x80\x9cThe only way you make Joe Biden sympathetic is to talk over him,\xe2\x80\x9d a G.O.P. operative said. \xe2\x80\x9cTrump\xe2\x80\x99s weakness in this debate is if he gets sucked into debating January 6 and who won the election. It\xe2\x80\x99s toxic to suburban voters in Bucks County and Chester County in Pennsylvania and Oakland County in Michigan.\xe2\x80\x9d

And yet, neither Republicans nor Democrats I spoke with envisioned an outcome in which this thing didn\xe2\x80\x99t go off the rails, at least for a moment or two. \xe2\x80\x9cIt will be an absolute food fight disaster,\xe2\x80\x9d the G.O.P. operative said. \xe2\x80\x9cIf they do some version of restraining, it probably lasts for the first 10 minutes, and then we see a food fight.\xe2\x80\x9d Another Democratic operative said: \xe2\x80\x9cOverwhelmingly [the debate] is likely to reinforce that people don\xe2\x80\x99t want this, and they\xe2\x80\x99re getting it, and they\xe2\x80\x99re going to stay home instead of vote. Trump\xe2\x80\x99s coalition is the easier managed of the two. He has the intensity of real fanaticism, but that\xe2\x80\x99s not enough. He needs that plus empathy. For Biden to make it about Trump is a higher lift. Biden cannot look feeble or lost. It will set off a Chernobyl-like reaction in the Democratic Party.\xe2\x80\x9d
FOUR STORIES WE\xe2\x80\x99RE TALKING ABOUT
Wet Hot British Summer
On the recent travails of WaPo and CNN\xe2\x80\x99s C.E.O.s.
DYLAN BYERS
Shari's Plan B
Plus, the Bezos-Lewis \xe2\x80\x9cThird Newsroom\xe2\x80\x9d master plan.
WILLIAM D. COHAN
Arnault\xe2\x80\x99s Richemont Toe Dip
Inspecting B.A.\xe2\x80\x99s newest investment.
LAUREN SHERMAN
A Hollywood Spy Saga
On the legal drama surrounding Yariv Milchan\xe2\x80\x99s Regency.
ERIQ GARDNER
